para.dtcname: Open in One Side of CAN Branch Line

content5.ptxt: If 2 or more ECUs and/or sensors do not appear on the intelligent tester "Communication Bus Check" screen, one side of the CAN branch wire may be open. (One side of the CAN-H [branch wire] / CAN-L [branch wire] of the ECU and/or sensor is open.)

entry.ptxt: 2 or more ECUs and/or sensors do not appear on the intelligent tester "Communication Bus Check" screen.

item.ptxt: One side of the CAN branch wire is open

atten4.ptxt: Perform the following inspection for the ECUs (sensors) which are not displayed on the intelligent tester. If the malfunction cannot be identified, then perform the following inspections for the ECUs (sensors) connected to CAN communication.

testgrp.testtitle: CHECK FOR OPEN IN ONE SIDE OF CAN BRANCH WIRE

test1.ptxt: Confirm the systems (ECUs and sensors), which use CAN communication, equipped on the vehicle. (See page )
Using the intelligent tester, select and perform "Communication Bus Check". (See page ) [*1]
Disconnect the connectors from the ECUs or sensors that are not displayed on the screen. [*2]
Check that only the ECUs and sensors from which the connectors were disconnected in the previous step are not displayed on the "Communication Bus Check" screen. [*3]

atten4.ptxt: If any ECUs or sensors, other than those from which the connectors were disconnected in the previous step, are not displayed on the "Communication Bus Check" screen, reconnect the disconnected connectors and repeat the procedures [*1], [*2], and [*3].

test1.ptxt: Perform the communication stop mode check for the ECUs and sensors which correspond to the disconnected connectors. (See page )

testgrp.testtitle: GO TO CORRESPONDING COMMUNICATION STOP MODE
